    Find A Grant
    Global Search Dialog
      1. Our Vision
      2. Focus Areas
      3. Health Leadership Development

      Strengthening Leadership for Health and Opportunity

      LEADERSHIP FOR BETTER HEALTH

      In communities around the nation, RWJF is supporting and developing leaders from different backgrounds and disciplines. We connect them with resources, tools, and one another for greater impact as they drive change. 

       

      Leadership that Addresses Structural Racism 

      Advancing health equity requires bold leaders who are working individually and collectively toward a society in which health is no longer a privilege, but a right.  Across the nation, a contingent of leaders in healthcare, community organizing, advocacy, and other fields are deeply committed to structural change and the long-term work of dismantling structural racism. These leaders come from varying backgrounds. Some hold formal titles, while others exercise informal influence. Some bring the wisdom of lived experience and others technical expertise. Working in a variety of fields from healthcare to social justice to academia, these leaders all share one vision: to change the unfair policies and practices that limit opportunity in their institutions and systems.  

      Focus Area

      Powering Leadership to Transform Health

      Investing in leaders who are changing policies and practices in healthcare and other systems so everyone has a fair chance at a healthy life.

      Read more

      Related News and Insights

      Read expert perspectives and the latest research from RWJF to explore the opportunities and complexities of this topic.

      Why Diversifying the Health Professions Matters for Everyone

      We are committed to fostering a diverse workforce so every person, regardless of who they are or where they come from, can access the care they deserve to live a healthier life.
      Read the blog post

      Strengthening the Healthcare Workforce With HBCUs

      HBCU medical school graduates are more likely to practice in primary care and to serve communities that lack healthcare. RWJF aims to increase support for greater collective action and to honor their contributions.
      Health Leadership Development Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

      Black Women’s Executive Directors Program

      The Black Women’s Executive Directors Program supports Black women-led racial and social justice organizations, offering leaders sustained general operating support, peer community, holistic leadership development, and wellness resources.
      Health Leadership Development Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

      How Philanthropy Can Help Position Leaders with Lived Experience for Success

      Leaders who have firsthand knowledge of their communities and understand their specific needs and strengths are known as proximate leaders. Philanthropy can support them in numerous ways.
      Equitable Community Development Community Power
      View all perspectives and research

      Stay informed

      Sign up for the latest funding alerts, news, research, and perspectives from RWJF.

      Email address already subscribed

      Email address already subscribed. Please check your inbox to manage your subscriptions.

      Subscribed!

      Thank you. You are now subscribed.

      Are you an RWJF Grantee (Past or Present?)
      Are you a European Union Resident?
      Tell us what type of content you want to receive. Select at least one (required).

      Be informed with our twice a month newsletter updating you with relevant news and research around a Culture of Health, as well as the latest funding opportunities.

      Get funded by RWJF: Receive notifications when new funding opportunities are released.

      Receive monthly updates on RWJF-sponsored research that informs many robust health policy debates on Capitol Hill, covering topics like health equity, improving access to quality healthcare, equitable housing, and more.

      Shop talk for researchers. This monthly newsletter covers research news and opportunities from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation.

      Communications expedite action. Get periodic research and expert insights on the best ways to communicate so we can spread what works.

      Sometimes we have news, announcements or opportunities that don't quite fit the subscription parameters above. If you're interested, we'll send you this information under "There's more...". *If you've indicated you are an EU resident, we will only send these communications if you intentionally check this box.
